description: FIXED-ATTACKER PvP ladder (2026-07-04, WOS_BattleReports_LadderCalibration_v9b.pdf). Attacker 沃军 FIXED at 10,000 (5,000 T10 inf + 5,000 T6 marks, Bradley only) EVERY battle; defender [RTS]Colonel Muller (T7 inf + T6 marks, 50-50) VARIES 3K/6K/10K/16K. No-proc (deterministic). All attacker VICTORY, defender wiped, attacker MARKS untouched (all attacker casualties = infantry). This is the decisive def_ed probe. 10K rung from v9 (10Kv10K).

analysis.casualty_exponent: attacker casualties 169/447/966/1835 vs defender 3K/6K/10K/16K -> casualties ~ N_def^1.42 (precise; log-log slope 1.42).
analysis.duration_exponent: Bradley S3 procs 2/3/4/6 -> ~8/12/16/24 turns -> T ~ N_def^0.66 (coarse, +-4 turns).
analysis.def_ed_naive: simple model casualties~N_def^(1+def_ed) -> def_ed~0.42 (FRONTAGE-LIMITED, near beast 0.48). CONTRADICTS the v9 symmetric ladder's constant-loss-fraction (which alone implied def_ed~1).
analysis.resolution: The contradiction means the kernel is a 2-COUNT structure: each side's kills/turn depend on BOTH its own count AND the enemy's count (target-abundance). From casualties(1.42) + duration(0.66): attacker target-abundance b~0.34, defender count-exponent d~0.76. The symmetric ladder's constant fraction is then consistent (self-similar). Single 'def_ed' is too simple; engine needs the 2-count terms.
analysis.verdict: The frontage/sublinear effect IS real in PvP (defender output sublinear in its count), plus a target-abundance term. This is NEW structure the pvp_engine lacks (currently attacker-linear a=1,b=0; defender N_def^def_ed, e=0).

next: MIRROR ladder to over-determine: FIX the defender (e.g. 6K), VARY the attacker 3K/6K/10K/16K -> isolates the attacker's own exponents (a, e) independently. Then fit the full 2-count kernel to all three ladders.
